title: Tender Flaky Ukrainian Boudin Noir Pilaf
description: A sweet and flaky Ukrainian dish featuring boudin noir, miso marinate, and low carb friendly ingredients, this pilaf is a nostalgic treat for the senses. It combines the richness of boudin noir with the savory flavor of miso and the comfort of a warm pilaf.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the miso paste and olive oil to create the marinate.
Add the sliced boudin noir to the marinate and toss to coat.
Let the boudin noir marinate for at least 30 minutes.
In a large skillet, saute the diced onion and minced garlic over medium heat until the onion is translucent.
Add the cauliflower rice to the skillet and cook until it is tender.
Add the marinated boudin noir, chicken broth, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the pilaf from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
